pr.title: refine splits and match and link itmaplib.c
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Split the remaining common-item data TU out of src/melee/it/it_2725.c into new matching src/melee/it/it_3F14.c, updated splits/symbols and dependent includes, and refined src/melee/it/itmaplib.c enough to mark it Matching. The PR moved it_802759DC from the previous ithitbox split range into itmaplib, added its prototype, adjusted data/sdata/sdata2/sbss ownership, and used localized matching tactics such as helper inlines, stack padding, volatile constant ordering, and selective dont_inline pragmas. Bot metrics reported +3072 matched code bytes, +8512 linked code bytes, +252 matched data bytes, +164 linked data bytes, and 13 new matches, while also flagging apparent split-boundary 'broken matches' for ithitbox/it_2725 caused by code/data relocation.
what_changed: Added a new split unit, src/melee/it/it_3F14.c, and assigned it .data 0x803F1418-0x803F1F00, .bss 0x804A0E30-0x804A0FD8, and .sbss 0x804D6D00-0x804D6D48 in config/GALE01/splits.txt.

Removed a large block of common-item tables and data declarations from src/melee/it/it_2725.c and placed them in src/melee/it/it_3F14.c, including it_803F1418, it_803F14C4, it_803F1ED8, it_803F1EE4, and it_803F1EF0.

Added src/melee/it/it_3F14.h with externs for common item data and sbss globals such as it_804A0E30, it_804A0E50, it_804A0E70, it_804D6D20, it_804D6D28, and typed helper structs it_804D6D20_t and it_804D6D40_t.
Updated many item and item-adjacent translation units to include it/it_3F14.h after data moved out of it_2725.h/it_2725.c.
Changed configure.py to add Object(Matching, "melee/it/it_3F14.c") and to upgrade Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/itmaplib.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/it/itmaplib.c").
Adjusted the split boundary for ithitbox.c and itmaplib.c: ithitbox text now ends at 0x802759DC and itmaplib text now starts at 0x802759DC.
Implemented or relocated it_802759DC into itmaplib.c and added its prototype to itmaplib.h.
Updated symbols.txt so many formerly address-style local objects in the affected ranges became compiler-style local labels such as @446, @556, @196, @197, @370, and sdata2 locals @260 through @155.
Corrected the symbol size for it_804D6D1C from size 0x1 to size 0x4 and represented it in it_3F14.h as extern u8 it_804D6D1C[4].
Refined itmaplib.c functions including it_8027649C, it_802765BC, it_80276934, it_80277040, it_8027737C, it_802775F0, and sqrt helper code to improve matching.
smart_moves: Separated pure/common data ownership from it_2725.c into it_3F14.c before trying to link itmaplib, reducing mixed-TU pressure and making split boundaries clearer.
Created it_3F14.h rather than leaving cross-TU data as ad hoc externs, then propagated the include to all users that depended on the moved common item tables/globals.
Kept item logic table entries and comment labels together in the new TU, preserving the common item index ordering while changing file ownership.
Recognized that the it_802759DC range belonged to itmaplib rather than ithitbox and moved the split boundary accordingly.
Used compiler-style local symbol names for local strings/constants in symbols.txt instead of forcing global it_80xxxxxx names where scope was local.

Matched itmaplib by factoring repeated collision-normal/angle logic into a static inline reference helper and calling the shared it_80276408 helper, instead of duplicating large branches in each caller.
Used targeted stack padding and variable reordering in itmaplib.c, including PAD_STACK and explicit u8 pad arrays, to satisfy remaining codegen differences.
Used volatile local constants in sdata2_ordering to force sdata2 constant order without giving those constants misleading semantic global names.
Replaced local assert string globals from it_2725 with literal assert strings like "translate" after moving the data TU, avoiding stale symbol dependencies.
Applied #pragma dont_inline only around specific functions where codegen required it, rather than globally constraining the whole file.
decomp_lessons: When a large TU contains data tables that actually form their own split, move the tables and their data/sbss ranges first; this can unblock matching/linking in neighboring code TUs.
Split-boundary changes may appear as broken matches in old units while still being correct overall; verify by checking the new owner unit and total matched/linked deltas.
A new data split needs synchronized changes across splits.txt, symbols.txt, headers, configure.py, and all dependent includes.
Local strings and constants should often be represented as local @NNN symbols in symbols.txt rather than stable global address names, especially once TU ownership is corrected.
Data size corrections in symbols.txt should be reflected in headers with matching C declarations; this PR changed it_804D6D1C to a 4-byte object and declared it as u8[4].
For matching repeated math/collision code, an inline helper can reproduce shared compiler structure better than hand-expanding similar code in each branch.
The decomp.dev report is useful for distinguishing real regressions from intentional ownership transfers across split ranges.
naming_conventions: New split file followed the project address-range placeholder naming pattern: it_3F14.c / it_3F14.h for data beginning near 0x803F1418.
Common item callback names retained item-specific LogicN naming, e.g. itCapsule_Logic0_Spawned, itTaru_Logic2_PickedUp, itBombhei_Logic6_Spawned.
Unknown data structs were named from their anchor symbol addresses, e.g. it_804D6D20_t and it_804D6D40_t, with offset-style fields x0, x4, x8, xC.
Local data/sdata/sdata2 symbols in symbols.txt used compiler local @NNN labels after the split refinement, while confirmed global data kept it_80xxxxxx names.
The new header guard used GALE01_IT_3F14.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved it_802759DC into itmaplib.c by changing the ithitbox/itmaplib text split boundary from 0x80275BC8 to 0x802759DC.
Added static f32 sdata2_ordering(void) with volatile f32/f64 locals to control constant ordering in .sdata2.
Used PAD_STACK and explicit padding arrays in itmaplib functions to align stack frame layout.
Introduced static inline f32 it_8027649C_ref(Item_GObj*, Vec3*, Vec3*) to share the angle-normal computation used by it_802765BC branches.
Refactored it_8027649C to call it_80276408 for collision normal selection rather than open-coding the env_flags checks.
Changed multiplication sequencing in it_80276934 by assigning intermediate angle values, likely to match instruction order.
Changed boolean/zero comparisons to explicit floating-point comparisons such as item1->x94.x != temp_f3 and item->spin_spd != temp_sqrt.
Inverted early-return structure in it_8027737C so the main body executes after checking item->xDC8_word.flags.x1F and item->xD5C.
Added sqrtf_accurate_local using __frsqrte Newton iterations and a volatile store, then used it through return_sqrt_value.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around it_80276CB8 and it_80277040.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 68.96% (+0.08%, +3072 bytes), linked code 41.78% (+0.22%, +8512 bytes), matched data 41.20% (+0.02%, +252 bytes), and linked data 34.94% (+0.01%, +164 bytes).
The bot listed 13 new matches including it_3F14 .data/.bss/.sbss at 100%, itmaplib it_802759DC, it_802765BC, it_8027737C, .data, .sdata2, it_80276934, and it_80277040.

The bot also flagged two broken matches and one unmatched-item regression in the previous owners: ithitbox it_802759DC, it_2725 .bss, and it_2725 .sbss; these align with the intentional split/data ownership moves.
follow_up_queries: Which remaining 19 item TUs and 79 item functions were left after PR #2549?
Audit all users of it_3F14.h to see whether some includes can later be replaced by narrower forward declarations.
Check later PRs for semantic renames of it_3F14.c globals and structs such as it_804D6D20_t and it_804D6D40_t.
Review whether sdata2_ordering remained necessary after subsequent item TU splits or symbol cleanups.
Look for later fixes around it_8027AB64_SelectKind in it_279C.c, where this PR added a disabled #if 0 alternative implementation.
